    Stay Informed

    Introduction

    Empowering the Next Generation of Researchers and Educators

    In higher education, fixed resources and rising AI-driven demand define the status quo. NVIDIA’s broad portfolio of tools, technologies, and programs connects labs, classrooms, and cloud into a powerful ecosystem, helping research teams scale complex simulations and turn bold hypotheses into high-impact results faster than ever.

    Emerging researchers access workshops, hackathons, and grants to turn curiosity into cutting-edge careers. Students and educators tap into curriculum, teaching kits, and hands‑on experiences that build practical expertise with modern accelerated computing. Together, we’re helping campuses evolve into engines of innovation—where every lab, classroom, and project can move beyond the status quo and advance at the speed of discovery.

    Upskilling for Emerging AI Educators

    Explore our diverse set of resources—including hands-on workshops, self-paced courses, certifications, webinars, blogs, live events, grants, and developer news.

    Learn More

    NVIDIA Academic Grant Program

    Advance your research with world-class computing resources. Now accepting proposals for academic research projects in a variety of topic areas.

    Apply Now

    Use Cases

    Discover How Higher Education Is Using AI

    Physical AI and Robotics

    Building intelligent robotic systems.

    Discover how advances in physical AI are making it easier for educators, students, and researchers to develop intelligent autonomous machines. Whether you're a beginner or an experienced researcher, NVIDIA’s full-stack, accelerated cloud-to-edge systems provide the tools you need to build and deploy advanced robotic systems.

    Continue Your Journey in Physical AI

    Life Sciences

    Advancing human health through academic innovation.

    NVIDIA provides open source tools, pretrained models, and GPU-accelerated pipelines for drug discovery, healthcare robotics, medical imaging, genomics, and digital health. With domain-specific platforms that combine AI models, tools, and infrastructure, NVIDIA helps researchers accelerate workloads from data ingestion to deployment.

    Learn About Key Applications of AI in Healthcare and Life Sciences

    Climate

    Elevating climate research with AI and high-performance computing (HPC).

    Advance climate research and revolutionize the fight against climate change with NVIDIA's AI and HPC technologies, including NVIDIA Earth-2 for advanced climate modeling and NVIDIA NIM™ microservices for optimizing real-world outcomes.

    Discover How HPC Can Transform Climate Science

    Quantum Computing

    Shaping the future of scientific discovery.

    Discover how NVIDIA Quantum integrates quantum and classical computing with AI, enhancing research and education through advanced algorithms, optimized workflows, and new applications in quantum computing.

    Learn More About NVIDIA Quantum

    Agentic AI

    Harness agentic AI to transform research and learning.

    Explore how agentic AI empowers educators, students, and researchers to work smarter by building, optimizing, and accelerating autonomous agents. Build and deploy intelligent, long-running agents with NVIDIA Nemotron™ open models and NVIDIA® NemoClaw™ reference architectures for simplifying secure, always-on agents.

    See Agentic AI in Action

    Telecommunications

    Accelerating the path to AI-native 6G.

    NVIDIA provides a full-stack, open source platform empowering researchers, developers, and students to design, simulate, and validate AI-native 6G networks with GPU-accelerated infrastructure to bring next-generation wireless to life faster than ever.

    Explore Telecommunications

    "Build It Yourself” Agentic AI Series

    Virtual training to equip you with essential insights and tools to advance your agentic AI expertise.

    Join us for this free virtual series and go hands-on with NVIDIA’s agentic AI stack. Move beyond traditional chatbots and learn to design autonomous agents that plan, reason, and act—from AI academic planners to collaborative multi-agent research teams. Powered by NVIDIA NemoClaw and OpenShell™. No specialized hardware required.

    Join for Free Today

    Success Stories

    See the Real-World Impact

    Learn how universities are adopting AI across various academic disciplines.

    Harvard Medical School Studies Protein Interactions at an Unprecedented Pace Using NVIDIA DGX Cloud

    The National Science Foundation’s National Artificial Intelligence Research Resource (NAIRR) pilot program with access to NVIDIA DGX™ Cloud, The Walter Lab at Harvard Medical School completed nearly 1.7 million protein interaction predictions in 3 months.

    Learn More

    Ohio State University Transforms Conservation Science With Biology Foundation Models

    Researchers at The Ohio State University are digitizing living organisms for broad science and conservation efforts through BioCLIP 2—a Tree of Life foundation model for half of the world’s named species. Built using NVIDIA A100 and H100 GPUs, it achieves the best or top-two performance for species identification and zero-shot recognition across almost one million taxa. The model unlocks emergent ecological and evolutionary insights and was recently featured at NeurIPS.

    Learn More

    Argonne National Laboratory Accelerates Cosmic Discovery With AI-Powered Framework

    Researchers at Argonne National Laboratory and collaborating institutions have developed RADAR, a federated, privacy-enhancing framework that enables observatories to coordinate gravitational-wave and radio follow-up without moving or exposing proprietary data. Running on NVIDIA-accelerated supercomputing systems, RADAR demonstrates how multi-messenger discovery can be accelerated through secure, cross-observatory workflows that preserve data ownership.

    Read More

    VinuesaLab Advances Urban Flow Simulations With AI Powered by NVIDIA PhysicsNeMo

    Researchers at the University of Michigan developed Diff-SPORT, which combines generative models with advanced inference to deliver scalable, interpretable urban flow simulations. The team merged computational fluid dynamics with AI platforms using NVIDIA PhysicsNeMo™, which provides the core infrastructure for diffusion models and physics-informed machine learning.

    Read More

    University of Maryland Researchers Develop Robots for Complex Household Tasks

    Researchers at the University of Maryland, recipients of a grant from the NVIDIA Academic Grant Program, are developing AI-powered humanoid systems capable of complex household tasks with greater autonomy. Using the NVIDIA Isaac™ open robotics development platform, they create photorealistic virtual home environments populated with diverse objects, allowing robots to practice millions of task variations and safely test rare or complex scenarios.

    Read More

    UC Santa Cruz Maps Coastal Flooding With NVIDIA Accelerated Computing

    UC Santa Cruz Center for Coastal Climate Resilience focuses on modeling and mapping the benefits of coral reefs and mangroves for reducing flood risk, which helps inform adaptation and preservation efforts. The center uses NVIDIA CUDA-X™ software, including the cuPyNumeric library and nvfortran compiler, running on NVIDIA RTX™ GPUs awarded through the NVIDIA Academic Grant Program.

    Read More

    Physicists Tap James Webb Space Telescope to Track New Asteroids and City-Killer Rock

    Smaller asteroids have been located farther out with the world’s most powerful space telescope, which can now also track the city crusher 2024YR4 asteroid with a 2.3% chance of hitting Earth.

    Read More

    BRLi and Toulouse INP Develop AI-Based Flood Models Using NVIDIA PhysicsNeMo

    A joint team at BRLi and National Polytechnic Institute of Toulouse (Toulouse INP), through a collaboration in the ANITI research institute, has designed an end-to-end AI approach that fully replaces the physics-based solver. This team includes expertise in both hydraulic modeling and AI methods for physics, enabling this interdisciplinary work. The Toulouse INP team then used these to train an end-to-end AI system to emulate the solver leveraging NVIDIA PhysicsNeMo from the Earth-2 platform.

    Read More

    Spinal Health Diagnostics Gets Deep Learning Automation

    Researchers used a deep learning model powered by an NVIDIA RTX A6000 GPU to automate spinal diagnostics, achieving 88% reliability for curvature predictions. The result: faster diagnoses, fewer errors, and better outcomes for patients with scoliosis and beyond.

    Read More

    The University of Florida Builds the Blueprint for the Future of AI on Campus

    The University of Florida’s AI infrastructure and supercomputer, the HiPerGator, is used to build a host of AI solutions to serve students, faculty, staff, and researchers. The university uses NVIDIA NIM microservices and Llama NIM on its HiPerGator supercomputer to bring a family of NaviGator chatbots providing capabilities of large language models (LLMs) to every facet of university life.

    Read More

    Colorado State University Develops Real-Time Hailstorm Forecasting Model Using StormScope

    Colorado State University (CSU) cross-departmental collaboration between the Department of Electrical and Computer Engineering, Atmospheric Science, and the Cooperative Institute for Research in the Atmosphere (CIRA) recently trained a specialized NVIDIA Earth-2 StormScope Nowcasting model for operational severe hailstorm prediction.

    Read More

    News and Events

    AI Education, Innovation, and Impact

    From West Texas to the World: Texas Tech Launches Next-Generation AI Infrastructure

    The Texas Tech University System (TTU System) recently announced a transformative partnership with NVIDIA to deploy next-generation accelerated computing infrastructure, positioning Texas Tech University at the forefront of artificial intelligence, high-performance computing, and advanced research.

    Read More About the Partnership

    Marine Biological Laboratory Explores Human Memory With AI and Virtual Reality

    Researchers at the Marine Biological Laboratory are using NVIDIA RTX GPUs and HP Z Workstations to study the molecular mechanisms of human memory. Combining the VR platform syGlass with custom AI tools, they’re helping scientists—and even high school interns—visualize billions of neurons to pinpoint memory proteins.

    Read More About the Partnership

    Cal Poly to Open Advanced AI Factory With NVIDIA Technology

    Cal Poly opened a $3M AI factory powered by four NVIDIA DGX B200 systems, delivering up to 3x training and 15x inference performance gains. Open to all students, faculty, and regional partners, it supports AI research in agriculture, aerospace, biotechnology, and advanced manufacturing.

    Read More About the Launch

    GTC Berlin

    The premier AI conference will be in Berlin this October, bringing together developers, researchers, and industry leaders to explore the next generation of AI infrastructure, and beyond. Get notified when registration opens to discover what’s next in AI.

    Learn More About the Event

    CVPR

    NVIDIA researchers share their latest groundbreaking innovations and see how NVIDIA Research is collaborating with CVPR members to deliver AI breakthroughs across the community.

    Learn More About the Event

    ISC

    At ISC, connect with scientists, engineers, and technology leaders to explore the future of high-performance computing. See the latest breakthroughs in artificial intelligence, high-performance computing, and quantum technologies, as well as what lies ahead.

    Learn More About the Event

    SIGGRAPH

    Join us at the premiere conference to see the latest on OpenUSD, generative AI, and the latest in graphics, simulation, robotics, and spatial computing.

    Learn More About the Event

    Japan Science and Technology Agency Develops NVIDIA-Powered Moonshot Robot for Elderly Care

    Japan’s Moonshot program is developing AIREC robots for elderly care—assisting with hygiene, meals, and daily tasks. Powered by NVIDIA Jetson Orin™ NX modules and trained with Isaac Sim™, the robots aim to support Japan’s aging population and free caregivers to focus on quality-of-life care.

    Read More

    AI Minister Takes the Stage With Global Tech Giants at University of Bristol Summit

    Over 250 delegates gathered at the University of Bristol’s Isambard Summit 2026, where UK AI Minister Kanishka Narayan highlighted how national AI infrastructure, including the UK AI Research Resource, is accelerating innovation in healthcare, clean energy, and high-growth startups.

    Read More About the News

    Miles College Announces “Access for All” Workforce Preparedness Initiative

    Miles College launched “Access for All,” supporting up to 750 underserved Alabama high school students with AI workforce training. Developed with NVIDIA and StudyFetch under the White House Pledge to America’s Youth, the program uses NVIDIA Academy and Deep Learning Institute content to build practical AI skills.

    Read More About the Initiative

    AI Foundation Model GluFormer Outperforms Clinical Standards in Forecasting Diabetes and Cardiovascular Risk

    GluFormer, developed by MBZUAI and the Weizmann Institute, predicts diabetes and cardiovascular risk from just 10–14 days of glucose data. It outperforms the clinical standard HbA1c, identifying 66% of future diabetes cases and 69% of cardiovascular-death events across 19 diverse global cohorts.

    Read More About the Study

    AI Powers Collaboration in Climate Predictions

    NUS researchers developed CondensNet, a neural network that fixes a key instability in hybrid AI-physics climate models. By correcting water vapor oversaturation during condensation, the model enables decade-long climate simulations that stay physically accurate, which is a long-standing challenge in the field.

    Read More About the Research

    How NVIDIA Leverages Higher Education to Drive Breakthrough Innovation

    Explore how NVIDIA drives innovation through higher education while offering universities computing resources, funding, internships, and research partnerships. The model gives NVIDIA access to cutting-edge ideas and top talent while advancing AI breakthroughs across key technology sectors.

    Read More About the Commitment

    Technology

    Advance Education and Research With NVIDIA

    Learn about the AI and HPC hardware, software, and networking solutions for academic institutions.

    NVIDIA DGX Systems

    Whether creating quality customer experiences, delivering better patient outcomes, or streamlining the supply chain, enterprises need infrastructure that can deliver AI-powered insights. Find out how NVIDIA DGX™ systems deliver the world’s leading solutions for enterprise AI infrastructure at scale.

    Learn More

    Limitless Cloud Compute

    NVIDIA platforms are powering next-generation capabilities in AI, HPC, and graphics, pushing the boundaries of what’s possible. Learn how innovators everywhere can access massive computing power on demand and with ease with NVIDIA’s GPU-accelerated solutions—available through all top cloud platforms.

    Learn More

    NVIDIA AI Enterprise

    NVIDIA AI Enterprise is an end-to-end, cloud-native suite of AI and data analytics software that’s optimized to enable any organization to use AI. It’s certified to deploy anywhere—from the enterprise data center to the public cloud—and includes global enterprise support to keep AI projects on track.

    Learn More

    Featured

    See All

    Teaching Kits

    NVIDIA DLI Teaching Kits lower the barrier of incorporating AI and GPU computing in coursework. They provide downloadable teaching materials and online courses that provide the foundation for understanding and building hands-on expertise in these critical areas.

    Download a Teaching Kit

    AI Essentials

    The NVIDIA AI Essentials Learning Series delivers getting-started resources for exploring the fundamentals of today’s hottest technologies.

    Learn the AI essentials from NVIDIA

    NVIDIA NIM and NVIDIA Blueprints

    NVIDIA NIM microservices offer the ability to run AI models on GPU instances, use free serverless APIs, or self-host on your own GPU infrastructure, while NVIDIA Blueprints provide guidelines for building and deploying your own AI applications.

    Try NVIDIA NIM APIs

    NVIDIA CUDA-Q Academic

    NVIDIA CUDA-Q™ Academic is an industry-aligned curriculum that prepares the next generation of quantum computing (QC) professionals to work with quantum-GPU supercomputing using CUDA-Q.

    Learn More About CUDA-Q Academic

    Hands-On AI Courses

    Learn AI by actually building it. NVIDIA’s self-paced courses run on real GPUs in your browser—no setup, no specialized hardware. Topics span generative AI, data science, robotics, and accelerated computing.

    Explore Courses

    NVIDIA Certifications

    Make your skills visible. Earn industry-recognized credentials in generative AI, AI infrastructure, data science, and OpenUSD—proof for employers that you can do the work, not just talk about it.

    Explore Certifications

    NVIDIA Student Network

    Check out this community engagement program that provides exclusive training, tools, and projects to students to help them build practical skills and jump-start a career in AI.

    Apply Now

    NVIDIA Developer Program

    Join the free program to gain access to SDKs, technical documentation, peer and domain expert help, and information on the right hardware to tackle your biggest challenges.

    Join Today

    Graduate Fellowship Program

    Apply to receive funding for your PhD research studies in accelerated computing, AI, and other related fields.

    Learn More

    Inception Program

    Gain access to cutting-edge technology, NVIDIA expertise, connections to venture investors, and co-marketing support to help your startup evolve faster.

    Learn More

    Tech Forums

    Connect with millions of like-minded developers, researchers, innovators, and NVIDIA technology experts.

    Learn More

    Jetson Community Forum

    Find answers to your questions about the NVIDIA Jetson™ platform in our open discussion forum.

    Learn More

    Jetson Community Projects

    Explore and learn from Jetson projects created by us and our community.

    Learn More

    NVIDIA Omniverse Community

    Join the NVIDIA Omniverse™ community, a global network of 3D creators and developers who are connecting and creating virtual worlds. Whether you're an expert or just getting started—take advantage of tutorials, challenges, resources, and more to guide and test your skills.

    Learn More

    NVIDIA Research

    Get to know NVIDIA Research. We’re passionate about developing the technology and finding the breakthroughs that bring positive change to the world. Beyond publishing our work in papers and at conferences, we apply it to NVIDIA solutions and services, share resources and code, and offer hands-on experiences with technical demos.

    Learn More

    Open Hackathons and Bootcamps

    Participate in our open hackathons and bootcamps to accelerate and optimize research applications with mentors by your side

    NVIDIA and our third-party partners use cookies and other tools to collect and record information you provide as well as information about your interactions with our websites for performance improvement, analytics, and to assist in our marketing efforts. By clicking "Accept All", you consent to our use of cookies and other tools as described in our Cookie Policy. You can manage your cookie settings by clicking on "Manage Settings". Please see our Privacy Policy for more information on our privacy practices.

    We have detected the Global Privacy Control (GPC) signal and recorded your rejection of all optional cookies on this site for this browser. You can manage your cookie settings by clicking on "Manage Settings". Please see our Cookie Policy for more information and visit our Privacy Policy for details on our privacy practices.

    Cookie Settings

    We and our third-party partners (including social media, advertising, and analytics partners) use cookies and other tracking technologies to collect, store, monitor, and process certain information about you when you visit our website. The information collected might relate to you, your preferences, or your device. We use that information to make the site work and with your consent to analyze performance and traffic on our website, to provide a more personalized web experience, and assist in our marketing efforts.

    Click on the different category headings below to find out more and change the settings according to your preference. You cannot opt out of Required Cookies as they are deployed to ensure the proper functioning of our website (such as prompting the cookie banner and remembering your settings, etc.). By clicking "Save and Accept" at the bottom, you consent to the use of cookies and other tools as described in our Cookie Policy in accordance with your settings. For more information about our privacy practices, please see our Privacy Policy.

    Required Cookies

    Always Active

    These cookies enable core functionality such as security, network management, and accessibility. These cookies are required for the site to function and cannot be turned off.

    Performance Cookies

    These cookies are used to provide quantitative measures of our website visitors, such as the number of times you visit, time on page, your mouse movements, scrolling, clicks and keystroke activity on the websites; other browsing, search, or product research behavior; and what brought you to our site. These cookies may store a unique ID so that our system will remember you when you return. Information collected with these cookies is used to measure and find ways to improve website performance.

    Personalization Cookies

    These cookies collect data about how you have interacted with our website to help us improve your web experience, such as which pages you have visited. These cookies may store a unique ID so that our system will remember you when you return. They may be set by us or by third party providers whose services we have added to our pages. These cookies enable us to provide enhanced website functionality and personalization as well as make the marketing messages we send to you more relevant to your interests. If you do not allow these cookies, then some or all of these services may not function properly.

    Advertising Cookies

    These cookies record your visit to our websites, the pages you have visited and the links you have followed to influence the advertisements that you see on other websites. These cookies and the information they collect may be managed by other companies, including our advertising partners, and may be used to build a profile of your interests and show you relevant advertising on other sites. We and our advertising partners will use this information to make our websites and the advertising displayed on it, more relevant to your interests.
